slots.meaning: The teen protagonist attempting the trial.
slots.guidance: A single capable, determined teen; never armed, never reckless.

slots.meaning: The generic singular noun for the trial's guide-figures and their trail-markings (used as 'the WARDEN marks', 'a WARDEN', 'the WARDENs').
slots.guidance: A person-type role (guide, judge, gatekeeper); never armed.

slots.meaning: Capitalized rank/title form of WARDEN, used only in the two named-success ending titles.
slots.guidance: A title of honor conferred on success; positive framing only.

slots.meaning: The proper name of the overarching one-day contest.
slots.guidance: A single formal event name; always preceded by fixed 'the'.

slots.meaning: The proper name of the whole forest/place the trial crosses; also closes one success-ending title.
slots.guidance: A single evocative place name; wonder over dread; no toxic hazard implied.

slots.meaning: The time of day the trial closes.
slots.guidance: A survivable time marker (e.g. a time of day); never framed as lethal.

slots.meaning: The recurring sound/signal marking the deadline approaching or passing (used bare and with a literal trailing 's').
slots.guidance: A audible, non-lethal signal; gives fair warning.

slots.meaning: The generic thorny-undergrowth material used as flavor in off-path / wrong-turn beats, regardless of which zone the hero is in.
slots.guidance: A benign-but-thick plant material; never itself lethal or toxic.

slots.meaning: The generic common noun for the kind of place the whole trial happens inside (used with fixed adjectives 'enchanted'/'darkening').
slots.guidance: A single common noun naming the setting's category (e.g. a bounded type of place); not a proper noun.

slots.meaning: The lesser, harder alternate route offered at the very end; also closes one success-ending title ('WARDEN_TITLE of the ALT_PATH').
slots.guidance: A named harder-but-honorable alternate route; positive framing.

slots.meaning: One of four approach paths into the trial's first zone.
slots.guidance: A passable, non-hazardous approach path; distinct from the other three.

slots.meaning: One of four approach paths into the trial's first zone.
slots.guidance: A passable, non-hazardous approach path; distinct from the other three.

slots.meaning: One of four approach paths into the trial's first zone.
slots.guidance: A passable, non-hazardous approach path; distinct from the other three.

slots.meaning: One of four approach paths into the trial's first zone.
slots.guidance: A passable, non-hazardous approach path; distinct from the other three.

slots.meaning: The formal in-prose name of zone 1 of 6 (used in beat/label text; zone 6's also closes one setback ending title).
slots.guidance: A short evocative place name for this checkpoint zone.

slots.meaning: The bare, ending-title short form of zone 1's name (article 'the'/'The' is fixed template text, not part of the value).
slots.guidance: A single-word (or short) proper-noun form used only in ending titles.

slots.meaning: The 'underfoot' ground-cover noun for zone 1, used in the repeated beat template.
slots.guidance: A single benign ground-material noun.

slots.meaning: The formal in-prose name of zone 2 of 6 (used in beat/label text; zone 6's also closes one setback ending title).
slots.guidance: A short evocative place name for this checkpoint zone.

slots.meaning: The bare, ending-title short form of zone 2's name (article 'the'/'The' is fixed template text, not part of the value).
slots.guidance: A single-word (or short) proper-noun form used only in ending titles.

slots.meaning: The 'underfoot' ground-cover noun for zone 2, used in the repeated beat template.
slots.guidance: A single benign ground-material noun.

slots.meaning: The adjective describing zone 2's warden-figures in its hub sentence.
slots.guidance: A short descriptive adjective for a person-type figure; never implies a weapon.

slots.meaning: The distinctive environmental feature named in zone 2's hub sentence.
slots.guidance: A short benign environmental noun phrase.

slots.meaning: The name of the warden-test administered in zone 2.
slots.guidance: A single abstract virtue/skill noun (e.g. a quality being tested).

slots.meaning: The formal in-prose name of zone 3 of 6 (used in beat/label text; zone 6's also closes one setback ending title).
slots.guidance: A short evocative place name for this checkpoint zone.

slots.meaning: The bare, ending-title short form of zone 3's name (article 'the'/'The' is fixed template text, not part of the value).
slots.guidance: A single-word (or short) proper-noun form used only in ending titles.

slots.meaning: The 'underfoot' ground-cover noun for zone 3, used in the repeated beat template.
slots.guidance: A single benign ground-material noun.

slots.meaning: The distinctive environmental feature named in zone 3's hub sentence.
slots.guidance: A short benign environmental noun phrase.

slots.meaning: The specific creature named in zone 3's hub sentence (compounded with WARDEN, e.g. 'CREATURE-WARDEN').
slots.guidance: A single real or fantastical animal name; never a weapon.

slots.meaning: The unique landmark object named in zone 4's hub sentence.
